message:
test(readme): integration test for Describe + Correlation/Covariance (fd90.190 / br-gdbwk)

Adds readme_describe_and_correlation_compiles_and_runs covering 10
statistical-summary APIs documented in README lines 607-637 that
previously had no integration coverage.

DataFrame:
- describe (default 8-row count/mean/std/min/25/50/75/max summary)
- describe_with_percentiles (custom quantile rows)
- describe_dtypes (numeric-only via "number" alias)
- corr (Pearson NxN matrix)
- corr_method ("spearman" rank correlation + "kendall" tau)
- cov (covariance matrix)
- corrwith (column-wise correlation against another DataFrame)

Series-level:
- corr (Series-to-Series Pearson; asserts r=1.0 for perfectly linear
  series, exercising the documented behavior)
- cov_with (Series-to-Series covariance)
- autocorr (lag=1)

Found via self-review on the last 3 commits — these 10 methods all exist
in fp-frame but had drifted out of integration coverage.

Verified: 29 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -424,6 +424,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-g90b","title":"fp-conformance: fixture FP-P2C-005 groupby_sum_order expected_series matches insertion-order, not pandas default","description":"Observed: tests::packet_filter_runs_groupby_packet (FP-P2C-005) — 1/4 case fails: groupby_sum_order_strict.

Mismatch: actual=[Utf8("a"), Utf8("b")] (sorted alphabetical, ddof pandas default), expected=[Utf8("b"), Utf8("a")] (insertion order).

pandas' DataFrame.groupby('key').sum() (no explicit sort=False) sorts the result index alphabetically. The fixture's expected_series uses insertion order [b, a] which would only match groupby(sort=False). No 'sort' parameter is encoded in the fixture or the harness for this operation, so the harness defaults to sort=True (pandas default), and fp-frame correctly produces sorted [a, b].

Fix: update fixture expected_series to the pandas-default sorted form: index [a, b], values [6.0, 4.0]. Preserves the sum-correctness check while aligning with the default-sort semantics actually exercised.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:53:03.862207260Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:54:39.940806211Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:54:39.938900444Z","close_reason":"shipped caa4106: fixture expected_series re-aligned to pandas-default sort (a,b)/(6.0,4.0). Also upgraded bare assert in packet_filter_runs_groupby_packet to include report payload in panic msg. Test now 4/4 cases pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-gdbwk","title":"fd90.190: Integration test for README Describe + Correlation/Covariance sections","description":"fd90.190: Add integration test for README "Describe" + "Correlation and Covariance" sections (lines 607-637).

Both sections together describe ~10 statistical-summary APIs with zero integration coverage:

DataFrame:
- describe (default 8-row summary)
- describe_with_percentiles (custom quantiles)
- describe_dtypes (numeric / object filtering)
- corr (Pearson default)
- corr_method ("spearman", "kendall")
- cov (covariance matrix)
- corrwith (column-wise correlation against another DataFrame)

Series-level:
- corr (Series-to-Series Pearson)
- cov (Series-to-Series covariance)
- autocorr(lag)

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (single new test, ~50 lines).
Time estimate: 30-40min.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:14:23.049688793Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:16:23.927498946Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T19:16:23.925333274Z","close_reason":"Added readme_describe_and_correlation_compiles_and_runs covering 10 statistical-summary APIs: DataFrame describe (8-row default), describe_with_percentiles (custom quantiles), describe_dtypes (number alias), corr (Pearson 3x3), corr_method (spearman + kendall), cov, corrwith. Plus Series-level corr (asserts r=1.0 for linear Series), cov_with, autocorr. All 29 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-gdg2","title":"fpd-sql schema introspection types","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add backend-neutral SQL schema introspection value types and a SqlConnection::table_schema hook, with SQLite implementation backed by PRAGMA table_info. This gives PostgreSQL/MySQL adapters a stable contract for column name/type/nullability/default/primary-key metadata without leaking backend row formats.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cod-pandas-3","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:39:20.677118373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:32:36.183532469Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:32:36.181711582Z","close_reason":"Scope completed in parallel via fd90.21 on cc-pandas-2: SqlConnection::table_schema(table, schema) -> Result<Option<SqlTableSchema>, IoError> trait method shipped with rusqlite override using PRAGMA table_info. SqlColumnSchema (name, declared_type, nullable, default_value, primary_key_ordinal, comment via fd90.35, autoincrement via fd90.37) and SqlTableSchema (table_name, columns) value types in fp-io. PostgreSQL/MySQL adapters can override with their information_schema queries. Tests: sql_table_schema_unknown_table_returns_none / _simple_table / _pk_notnull_default / _schema_silently_ignored_on_sqlite / _rejects_invalid_table_name / _default_impl_returns_none / _routes_schema_to_backend_override. cod-pandas-3 was stalled since 2026-04-25 (cod agents at usage limits until 2026-04-30); closing to reflect actual completion.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backend-abstraction","fd90","schema","sql"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-gdg2","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:39:20.677118373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-gdrz5","title":"fd90.115: README sample(usize, ...) wants Option<usize>; sample_weights wants &[f64] not &Series","description":"fd90.74-114 README audit. Random Sampling section (lines 1616-1628) has multiple type mismatches:

1. df.sample signature at crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:22519:
     pub fn sample(&self, n: Option<usize>, frac: Option<f64>, replace: bool, seed: Option<u64>)
   But all three README calls pass raw integers for n:
     line 1618: df.sample(100, None, false, Some(42))?
     line 1621: df.sample(0, Some(0.1), false, Some(42))?
     line 1624: df.sample(1000, None, true, Some(42))?
   None of these compile — n must be Option<usize>, so Some(100), None (when using frac), Some(1000).

2. df.sample_weights signature at line 22591:
     pub fn sample_weights(&self, n: usize, weights: &[f64], replace: bool, seed: Option<u64>)
   But README at line 1627 passes &weights_series:
     df.sample_weights(100, &weights_series, false, Some(42))?
   weights wants &[f64], not &Series. Would fail compile.

Fix:
- Line 1618: 100 → Some(100)
- Line 1621: 0 → None (since frac is supplied)
- Line 1624: 1000 → Some(1000)
- Line 1627: &weights_series → &weights_vec or note that callers pre-extract into Vec<f64>

Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:57:42.179345297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:58:55.871142912Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:58:55.868580066Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
